section.title: Introduction
section.para: A stemmer reduces a word form to a shorter root. OpenNLP ships rule-based engines such as Snowball and Porter through the opennlp.tools.stemmer.Stemmer interface. Stemming is language-specific and lossy: it is useful for search and feature reduction, not as a substitute for lemmatization when a dictionary form is required.

section.title: StemmerFactory
Sharing and caching
section.para:
Some stemmer implementations keep mutable engine state and must not be called from several threads at once. SharingStemmer wraps a StemmerFactory and routes each call to a per-thread delegate, so one instance can be shared safely. CachingStemmer adds a bounded per-thread LRU cache of word-to-stem mappings on top of the same factory pattern. Both wrappers return the same stem as a fresh factory stemmer for the same word:
StemmerFactory factory = new SnowballStemmerFactory(SnowballStemmer.ALGORITHM.ENGLISH);
new SharingStemmer(factory).stem("running"); // "run" new CachingStemmer(factory).stem("running"); // "run"
Thread-safe stemmers such as the shareable SnowballStemmer class do not need SharingStemmer. Long-running containers should call clearThreadLocalState() when a pooled thread no longer uses a sharing or caching stemmer.

section.title: Hunspell dictionaries
section.para:
opennlp.tools.stemmer.hunspell is a clean-room engine over the documented Hunspell dictionary format: a user-supplied .aff affix file and its .dic word list. OpenNLP bundles no dictionary data, so each dictionary's own license stays with the files you download. The dictionary is immutable and safe to share; HunspellStemmerFactory hands out a fresh stemmer per call. HunspellManualExampleTest asserts the behavior shown here.
HunspellDictionary dictionary = HunspellDictionary.load( Path.of("en_US.aff"), Path.of("en_US.dic")); Stemmer stemmer = new HunspellStemmerFactory(dictionary).newStemmer();
stemmer.stem("workers"); // "work" stemmer.stem("table"); // "table" (unknown vocabulary is unchanged)
The in-tree test uses a project-authored miniature dictionary instead of a published one, and asserts the same stems for workers and worker. Acquisition helpers and the supported affix feature set live in opennlp-core/opennlp-runtime/dev/README-hunspell-dictionaries.md.
